title: Microdata and HTML Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential attributes for creating structured data in your HTML.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of 'Microdata' in HTML?
options: To make a web page load faster.
To provide a detailed meaning and context for existing data to search engines.
To define the page's color scheme and layout.
To automate form submission on the page.

questions.question: Which attribute is used to define the scope of a specific item (e.g., a person or product) in Microdata?
options: itemscope
itemtype
itemprop
itemname

questions.question: What is Schema.org?
options: A search engine specifically for HTML code.
A collaborative vocabulary used by search engines to understand Microdata.
A tool for automatically generating CSS styles.
A script used to track website traffic.

questions.question: Which Microdata attribute should you use to label a specific property like a person's name or a product price?
options: itemscope
itemtype
itemprop
itemval

questions.question: What is a 'Rich Snippet' in search result pages?
options: An extra link that redirects to a different website.
An enhanced search result that shows extra data like prices, ratings, or images.
A snippet of code that users can copy to their own sites.
A popup that appears when a user clicks on a search result.
